questions.question: What is an Entity type in Conceptual Data Modelling?
options: A) A set of related data
B) A single record
C) An abstract concept with properties
D) A physical storage device
questions.explanation: An entity type is an abstract concept with properties that define the characteristics of an object in a database.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a Weak Entity Type?
options: A) Customer
B) Order
C) Address
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: A weak entity type is an entity type that has a partial dependency on another entity type.

questions.question: What is the purpose of an ER Diagram in Conceptual Data Modelling?
options: A) To store data
B) To define relationships between entities
C) To create a physical database
D) To design a user interface
questions.explanation: An ER diagram is used to visually represent the relationships between entities and to identify structural constraints.

questions.question: What is an Entity Set in Conceptual Data Modelling?
options: A) A single instance of an entity
B) A group of related entities
C) An abstract concept with properties
D) A physical storage device
questions.explanation: An entity set is a group of related entities that have a common set of attributes.

questions.question: What is a structural constraint in Conceptual Data Modelling?
options: A) A rule that defines the values of an attribute
B) A rule that defines the relationships between entities
C) A rule that defines the data types of attributes
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: A structural constraint is a rule that defines the data types of attributes, the relationships between entities, or the values of attributes.
